niratthakena.meaning: useless; vain, unprofitable (instrumental: with what is useless)

niraya.meaning: hell; the lowest of the woeful planes of existence
breakdown: Going (from √i "to go"); niraya is the state of "going down", the downfall.
niraya.goenka: In the study material niraya, hell, is explained as the lowest of the apāyabhūmi, the fields of woeful existence, which comprise tiracchānayoni (the field of animal life), pettivisayo (the sphere of the petas), asurakāyo (the "body" of the asuras) and hell itself. According to the commentaries there are various stages of suffering and accordingly different hells. Klaus Nothnagel refers readers to lesson 3.2.3, Dhammacakkappavattanasuttaṁ, Part Two — The Four Noble Truths Have to Be Fully Realized, for the cosmology of the 31 planes of existence.

nirayaṁ.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular of niraya
breakdown: Going (from √i "to go"); the downfall, the woeful state.

nirodhanissitaṁ.meaning: dependent on cessation; supported by the elimination (of craving)
breakdown: Dependent on, leaning on, hanging on (past participle of nissayati).

nirodhasaccaniddeso.meaning: the exposition of the truth of cessation
nirodhasaccaniddeso.summary: This is a compound naming a section of text: "cessation + truth + exposition".
breakdown: Cessation (of suffering); the Third Noble Truth.

nirodhāya.meaning: for cessation; for the sake of the elimination (of suffering)
nirodhāya.gram: masculine noun, dative singular of nirodha
breakdown: To obstruct, stop; nirodha is cessation, extinction.
Dative singular ending: "for the sake of".

nirujjhamānā.gram: present participle (passive) of nirujjhati (ni + √rudh), plural or feminine form
breakdown: To cease, be dissolved (passive of nirundhati, ni + √rudh).

nirūpadhi.meaning: free from attachment; without the substrata of existence
breakdown: Attachment, acquisition; the substratum that supports continued existence (upa + √dhā).
To sit; the gerund nisajja means "having sat down".

nisajjā.meaning: sitting down; the act of sitting

nisajjā sitting.note: uncertain: this back-glossary row duplicates the entry nisajjā; the English word "sitting" was carried into the headword during extraction. See nisajjā.

nisaṁsaṁ.meaning: benefit; advantage (from the acquisition of many qualities)
nisaṁsaṁ.gram: noun, accusative singular (truncated form of ānisaṁsa)
nisaṁsaṁ.note: uncertain: the lexicon row glosses the full compound anekaguṇapaṭilābhānisaṁsaṁ, "the benefit of acquiring many qualities"; the headword nisaṁsaṁ is the final element with its first syllables absorbed by sandhi.

nisedheyyaṁ.meaning: I should prevent; would stop, would put an end to
nisedheyyaṁ.gram: verb, 1st person singular optative of nisedheti (ni + √sidh)
breakdown: To ward off; nisedheti means to prevent, stop.
First person singular optative ending: "I should, I would".

nisinno.gram: past participle of nisīdati (ni + √sad), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: To sit; the past participle nisinna means seated.
Prefix: without. (The doubled ss arises by assimilation.)

nissāya.meaning: leaning on; dependent upon; by reason of, on account of, through
nissāya.gram: gerund of nissayati (ni + √si), used as a preposition
breakdown: To lean; nissayati means to lean on, depend on; the gerund nissāya functions like "depending on, because of".

nisāmetha.gram: verb, 2nd person plural imperative of nisāmeti (causative of ni + √sam)
breakdown: To be calm, attend; the causative nisāmeti means to listen, observe carefully.

nisīdeyya.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of nisīdati (ni + √sad)

nisīdi.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of nisīdati (ni + √sad)

nisīdiṁsu.gram: verb, 3rd person plural aorist of nisīdati (ni + √sad)

nivedetvā.meaning: having made known; having communicated, having announced
breakdown: To make known, announce (causative of ni + √vid "to know").

niveseti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of nivisati (ni + √vis)
breakdown: To enter, settle; the causative niveseti means to establish, cause to settle.

nivisamānā.gram: present participle of nivisati (ni + √vis), feminine nominative singular

niviṭṭho.meaning: settled; fixed on, bent on, devoted to, established
niviṭṭho.gram: past participle of nivisati (ni + √vis), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: To enter, settle; the past participle niviṭṭha means settled, established.

nivāreti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of ni + √var
breakdown: To obstruct, restrain; nivāreti means to ward off, prevent.

nivāsetvā.meaning: having dressed; having put on the (under-)robe
breakdown: To dress, put on clothing (causative of ni + √vas "to wear").

nivātavutti.meaning: of humble behaviour; humble in conduct
nivātavutti.summary: This is a compound, literally "without wind + conduct": behaving without inflation, humbly.

breakdown: Wind; nivāta is literally "windless", hence unpuffed-up, humble.
nivāto.goenka: Nivāto, humility, appears in the Maṅgalasutta among the highest welfares (maṅgala): together with gārava (respect), santuṭṭhi (contentment) and kataññutā (gratitude), it belongs to the qualities of conduct that the tradition presents as protections and blessings for one walking the path.

niyatā.gram: past participle of niyameti (ni + √yam), plural or feminine form
breakdown: To restrain, fix; niyata means fixed, certain, assured.
One's own (related to nija, from √jan "to be born").

niyyanti.meaning: they are led; they go out (to liberation)
niyyanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, passive of neti (√nī)
breakdown: To lead; the passive niyyati means "is led", also "goes out, is delivered".
Prefix: out. (The doubled yy arises by assimilation.)
To go; niyyāna is the going out, deliverance.

niyāmaṁ.gram: masculine abstract noun, accusative singular (from niyamati, ni + √yam)
breakdown: To restrain, fix; niyāma is the fixed method, the certain way.

niṭṭhaṅgato.meaning: having reached completion; come to certainty, arrived at the conclusion
niṭṭhaṅgato.gram: past participle compound (niṭṭhaṁ gata), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Gone to, come to (past participle of gacchati). (The ṅ arises by niggahīta sandhi before g.)

no cedaṁ.meaning: and if this were not so; but not this
no cedaṁ.gram: phrase: negative particle + sandhi of ca + idaṁ

nupanayhanti.meaning: they do not bind up (grudges); they do not nurse a grudge
nupanayhanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, negated (na + upanayhati, √nah)
breakdown: Not (its vowel absorbed by sandhi with the following u-).
To tie, bind; upanayhati means to tie up, harbour enmity, nurse a grudge.

nāccogāḷhaṁ.meaning: not too abundant; not excessively plentiful; not plunging too deep
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).
Plunged into, immersed (ava + √gāh); hence "gone too deep, too abundant".

nāccuṇhāya.gram: adjective, oblique feminine singular (na + ati + uṇha)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).

nādhigacchanti.meaning: they do not attain; they do not come into possession of
nādhigacchanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, negated (na + adhigacchati, √gam)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).
To go; adhigacchati means to attain, come into possession of.

nādhivāseti.meaning: does not endure; does not tolerate, does not consent to
nādhivāseti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative, negated (na + adhivāseti)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).
To endure, tolerate; also to consent (causative of adhi + √vas "to dwell").

nāgataṁ.meaning: the future (literally "what has not yet come")
nāgataṁ.gram: past participle, negated, accusative/neuter singular (na + āgata)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following ā- to nā-).
Come, arrived (past participle of āgacchati); nāgata is "the not-yet-come", the future.
Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).

nāmagottaṁ.meaning: name and clan; name and lineage

nāmetaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion: emphatic particle + pronoun (nāma + etaṁ)

breakdown: Various, different. (The doubled pp is compound sandhi doubling.)

nānattakathaṁ.meaning: desultory talk; gossip about this and that, all sorts of idle talk

nānubyañjanaggāhī.meaning: not grasping at the details; not taking up the secondary characteristics (of sense objects)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).
Secondary characteristic, detail, particular feature (anu "following" + byañjana "mark").
Taking, seizing (from √gah; the doubled gg is compound sandhi doubling).
nānubyañjanaggāhī.goenka: Like na nimittaggāhī, this term belongs to the teaching on sense restraint (indriyasaṁvara): having seen a form or cognized an object, the practitioner does not grasp at its secondary details — the features around which craving and aversion would otherwise multiply at the sense doors.

nānujaññā.meaning: should not permit; would not approve, would not consent to
nānujaññā.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative, negated, irregular (na + anujānāti, √ñā)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).
To know; anujānāti means to permit, approve. The optative anujaññā is an irregular contraction of anujāneyya.
